original_abstract: In the era of globalization and rapid digitalization, the Christian church faces a massive influx of information and religious content that often challenges traditional orthodoxy. Contemporary heretical teachings, which distort biblical truths, have become increasingly complex, threatening social stability, the unity of the Body of Christ, and the foundational spirituality of individual believers. This study explores the theological challenges of identifying and overcoming modern heresies, emphasizing the church's vital role in safeguarding the integrity of the faith. Utilizing a descriptive qualitative method and a literature study approach, the research analyzes the nature of contemporary heresy through the lens of spiritual reality. The findings suggest that the church must act as a primary guardian of truth by providing in-depth theological education and actualizing its defensive role against misleading influences. Ultimately, the paper underscores the necessity for the church to equip its congregation with the discernment required to navigate the spiritual complexities of the digital age, thereby maintaining the unity and purity of the Christian message.
humanized_abstract: With the influx of globalisation and digitalisation the Christian church is flooded with vast amounts of information and religious literature which challenge orthodox doctrine. New age teachings which distort biblical truths have become increasingly complex and therefore pose a great challenge to societal stability, the unity of the Body of Christ and to the spiritual foundation of individual Christians. This study deals with the problems faced by the church in relation to the identification and combating of heresies in the modern age in relation to the preservation of the faith. The descriptive qualitative method with literature study was used in the research in order to study the contemporary heresies from the perspective of spiritual reality. The results of the study show that the church as the main preserver of faith should take action in order to guarantee an in-depth theological training for the Christians, and that the church must make real the defensive role of preserving the faith and protecting the believers from being misled. The study also underlines the importance of the church's role in providing the Christians with enough discernment to cope with the spiritual challenges of the digitalisation in order to maintain the unity and purity of the Christian faith.
